does: ForgeDAN presents an evolutionary algorithm framework for automatically generating adversarial prompts that jailbreak aligned large language models. The method introduces multi-strategy textual perturbations operating at character, word, and sentence levels to create diverse mutation candidates. It employs a semantic fitness evaluation mechanism using text similarity models to guide evolution toward effective attacks, and implements a dual-dimensional jailbreak judgment system that combines an LLM-based classifier with compliance and harmfulness assessment to reduce false positives. The framework aims to generate semantically coherent prompts that reliably bypass safety mechanisms in aligned models.
connects: This work builds on prior automated jailbreak research, particularly extending approaches like AutoDAN that use evolutionary strategies to craft adversarial prompts. ForgeDAN addresses known limitations in existing methods including shallow fitness evaluation relying on keyword matching and limited mutation diversity. The paper engages with broader literature on LLM alignment and safety, adversarial attack generation in NLP, and the arms race between attack and defense mechanisms in AI systems. It draws on evolutionary algorithm techniques and incorporates semantic evaluation methods that go beyond simple surface-level pattern matching used in earlier jailbreak detection systems.
enables: This framework advances the technical capability to systematically test LLM alignment defenses, establishing stronger benchmarks for evaluating safety mechanisms. The multi-strategy perturbation approach and semantic fitness evaluation could inform more robust defense mechanisms that account for semantic coherence rather than keyword filtering. Future work could apply similar evolutionary and semantic-evaluation techniques to other security testing domains for language models. The dual-dimensional judgment methodology provides a template for more nuanced evaluation of harmful outputs, potentially supporting development of better adversarial robustness metrics and defenses that understand attack intent rather than surface patterns.
